data.title: Xero's Bull Sees Anthropic Partnership as a Positive — Market Talk
data.description: Xero's bull at Citi sees the accounting-software provider's partnership with Anthropic as an incremental positive for the stock. While not expecting any disclosure of the partnership's unit economics, analyst Siraj Ahmed tells clients in a note that the collaboration is in line with Xero's strategy of using AI assistants as a distribution channel. While he wonders exactly what customer-facing changes might occur, Ahmed continues to see Xero's app ecosystem as an advantage. Citi has a last-published buy rating and A$144.80 target price on the stock, which is at A$72.40 ahead of the open. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Canada's Westaim net loss narrows in Q4 as asset management unit turns positive
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada integrated insurance and asset manager's Q4 net loss narrowed YoY

  • Asset Management segment posted positive adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025

  • Insurance segment continued building MYGA annuity issuance and expanded agent network

Outlook

  • Company expects agent onboarding and policy applications to continue to grow in 2026

  • Westaim says annuity policy issuance is pacing close to previous guidance

  • Company plans continued growth of Arena’s business in 2026 and beyond

Result Drivers

  • ASSET MANAGEMENT FEES - Asset Management segment generated $15.9 mln in fee revenues and performance allocations, contributing to positive adjusted EBITDA

  • ANNUITY PRODUCT - Insurance segment continued growing MYGA annuity issuance, resulting in premium generation and agent network expansion

  • PLATFORM BUILD-OUT COSTS - Insurance segment's adjusted EBITDA loss included $0.7 mln in platform build-out expenses

Company press release:

data.title: Constellation Energy Exec Says Reactor Restart Faces Grid Bottleneck — WSJ
data.description:
By Jennifer Hiller

A Constellation Energy executive said on Thursday that a reactor at the former Three Mile Island nuclear power plant will be ready to generate electricity again in 2027, but the company was told that it might not be able to connect to the power grid until 2031.

Constellation and Microsoft struck a deal in 2024 to restart the undamaged reactor at the Pennsylvania site, now called the Crane Clean Energy Center. A 20-year power-purchase agreement is spurring the restart.

Grid operator PJM Interconnection told Constellation about the potential years-long delay after a study, said David Dardis, senior executive vice president at Constellation, in a presentation at a global energy conference in Houston.

"That's crazy," Dardis said. "That thing's been on the grid for decades operating and now we can't get on until 2031."

Constellation is working with PJM, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and the transmission utility to try to accelerate the timeline, Dardis added.

At the same event at S&P Global's CERAWeek energy conference, Amanda Peterson Corio, Google's global head of data-center energy, said that in Virginia, Google has been told it could face wait times of up to 7 to 10 years to have a project studied. Getting electric service to a site could take longer than two decades, she said, which has Google and other firms considering projects in other states instead.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.description:
